title: VolcanoPlotR
text: VolcanoPlotR way generate nice volcano plots MaxQuant data. MaxQuant proteinGroups.txt placed Data directory, can run following code generate volcano plot: asked groups want compare. Proteins enriched Group1 towards right plot enriched Group2 towards left. example file included package, can run following code see works:  output volcano plot can customised . See vignettes information. Changing colours plot appearance, including adding labels proteins interest (vignette("appearance")) Text output enriched proteins, alternative statistical tests processing options (vignette("outputs")) Combining MaxQuant data multiple experiments generating volcano plot combined data (coming soon)
code: library(VolcanoPlotR) workflow_maxquant() library(VolcanoPlotR) # get the path to the proteinGroups.txt file included in the package filepath <- system.file("extdata", "proteinGroups.txt", package = "VolcanoPlotR") # get the filename from the path filename <- basename(filepath) # get the directory name filedir <- dirname(filepath) # run the automated procedure we will also tell it which groups to compare so # we don't have to select them interactively workflow_maxquant(file = filename, datadir = filedir,                   group1 = "WT", group2 = "Control") #> Using specified groups: WT versus Control

title: Changing Volcano Plot Appearance
text: change colour points volcano plot, can use vp_colours parameter volcano_plot_maxquant() function. default colours set specific palette, can change colours like.  first thing understand volcano plot divided “sectors”. sectors defined thresholds p-value fold change. default thresholds 0.05 p-value 1 fold change, divides plot 6 sectors (named 0 5). sectors defined follows: Sector 0: p > 0.05 |log2FC| < 1 Sector 1: p <= 0.05 |log2FC| < 1 Sector 2: p > 0.05 log2FC <= -1 Sector 3: p <= 0.05 log2FC <= -1 Sector 4: p <= 0.05 log2FC >= 1 Sector 5: p > 0.05 log2FC >= 1 visualise :  Knowing sectors ’d like colour differently, can specify colours sector using vp_colours parameter volcano_plot_maxquant() function. colours specified named vector, names correspond sector numbers (0 5) values colours want use. Another example changing colours points volcano plot shown . also alter alpha, size shape points.
code: library(VolcanoPlotR) # load and process the example file filepath <- system.file("extdata", "proteinGroups.txt", package = "VolcanoPlotR") # get the filename from the path filename <- basename(filepath) # get the directory name filedir <- dirname(filepath) df <- load_maxquant(file = filename, datadir = filedir) df <- process_maxquant(df, group1 = "WT", group2 = "Control") #> Using specified groups: WT versus Control volcano_plot_maxquant(df, vp_colours = c("0" = "#a0a0a0", "1" = "#808080",                                          "2" = "#606060", "3" = "#00ddff",                                          "4" = "#606060", "5" = "#ff0000")) volcano_plot_maxquant(df, vp_colours = c("0" = "#a0a000", "1" = "#808080",                                          "2" = "#606060", "3" = "#00dd80",                                          "4" = "#606060", "5" = "#800080"),                       point_args = list(size = 2,                                         shape = 15,                                         alpha = 0.8))

title: Changing Volcano Plot Appearance
text: related concept changing thresholds p-value fold change. can done using threshold_p threshold_fc parameters volcano_plot_maxquant() function. default thresholds 0.05 p-value 1 fold change, can change values like. colouring automatically adjusted lines demarcate plots can modified using p_line, zero_line x_line parameters volcano_plot_maxquant() function.
code: volcano_plot_maxquant(df, threshold_p = 0.01,                                   threshold_fc = 2,                                   p_line = FALSE,                                   zero_line = FALSE,                                   x_line = TRUE)

title: Changing Volcano Plot Appearance
text: Adding labels proteins interest done using label_points parameter volcano_plot_maxquant() function. default label points, can label points, top n points, top n points specific sector, specify proteins interest label.
code: # rather than the default: volcano_plot_maxquant(df) we will (try to) label all proteins volcano_plot_maxquant(df, label_points = "all") # or we can label the top 20 volcano_plot_maxquant(df, label_points = "top_20") # or we can label the top 10 proteins in sector 5 only volcano_plot_maxquant(df, label_points = "5_10") # labelling all proteins in sector 5 is done like this volcano_plot_maxquant(df, label_points = "5_all") # to label specific proteins we can use a character vector of protein names, e.g. volcano_plot_maxquant(df, label_points = c("TPD52", "Q6RW13", "Synaptogyrin-2")) # they can be either a Gene.names, Protein.names, Protein.ID or a mix but whatever value you give will be used to label the protein. Note, it must be an exact match. # if you'd like to customise the labels, you can pass a list of arguments to the label_args parameter, e.g. volcano_plot_maxquant(df, label_points = c("TPD52", "Q6RW13", "Synaptogyrin-2"), label_args = list(size = 4, colour = "red", fontface = "bold"))

title: Analyzing multiple MaxQuant files with VolcanoPlotR
text: Using process_multiple_maxquant() list data frames, given option specifying group(s) corresponds group1 (right side volcano plot) group(s) corresponds group2 (left side volcano plot). function combine data files return single data frame can used plotting. simple example, two MaxQuant files two groups , “WT” “Control”. runs files called WT.1, WT.2, WT.3, Control.1, Control.2, Control.3. function presents user list groups found files asks group(s) used group1 group(s) used group2. specify WT group1 Control group2, function combine . rename runs test.1, test.2, test.3, test.4, test5, test.6, control.1, control.2, control.3, control.4, control.5, control.6 avoid duplicate names. complicated example, another set, groups called “WT” “Control” one file “wildtype” “control” . user can select list specify groups group1 group2. see list 1 WT, 2 Control, 3 wildtype, 4 control. select “1 3” group1 “2 4” group2, function combine rename runs test.1, test.2, test.3, test.4, test5, test.6, control.1, control.2, control.3, control.4, control.5, control.6. Note group names need unique logical, example WT Control mislabelled one file, names shared group1 group2 combined. solution rerun MaxQuant appropriate labelling. examples, user can bypass selection step specifying groups function call, using load_multiple_maxquant(group1 = "WT", group2 = "Control") first example load_multiple_maxquant(group1 = c("WT", "wildtype"), group2 = c("Control", "control")) second example. additional argument can used use average ratios rather ratio averages. useful datasets combined differing intensities. Using ratio = TRUE compare test.1 control.1, test.2 control.2, etc. average ratios. Using ratio = FALSE average test samples average control samples compare averages (option single file case). default ratio = FALSE.
code: library(VolcanoPlotR) my_list <- load_multiple_maxquant() df_subset <- process_multiple_maxquant(data = my_list) # or to bypass the selection step, specify the groups in the function call df_subset <- process_multiple_maxquant(data = my_list, group1 = c("WT", "wildtype"), group2 = c("Control", "control"))

title: Analyzing multiple MaxQuant files with VolcanoPlotR
text: next steps single MaxQuant file used. Use volcano_plot_maxquant() pca_plot_maxquant() mean_plot_maximum() generate plots. process_multiple_maxquant() can used input either functions.

title: Outputs from VolcanoPlotR
text: generate text output enriched proteins, can set text_output parameter TRUE volcano_plot_maxquant() function. generate text file containing enriched proteins associated statistics. output saved tab-separated text file text_output_dir directory (default “Output/Data/”). contains ranked list proteins ranking calculated manhattan distance origin (0,0) volcano plot. list can useful applications generating table publication.

what: Changing the processing of MaxQuant data
title: Outputs from VolcanoPlotR
text: default, process_maxquant() analysis LFQ intensity values. use different measurement, meas parameter can set one measurements MaxQuant output (e.g. “iBAQ”, “Intensity”, “MS/MS.counts”). baseval parameter can set value 0 use different base value log2 transformation. width downshift parameters can set different values change imputation missing values. seed parameter can set different value change random seed used imputation.

title: Outputs from VolcanoPlotR
text: standard method calculating p-values via unpaired two sample t-test assuming equal variance. change paired t-test, can set paired parameter TRUE process_maxquant() function, similarly var.equal parameter can set FALSE use Welch’s t-test instead Student’s t-test. default settings mirror Perseus processing.

title: Outputs from VolcanoPlotR
text: function mean_plot_maxquant() can used generate mean vs mean plot two groups compared. useful visualising distribution data. plot generated similar parameters volcano plot. examples shown :
code: library(VolcanoPlotR) # get the path to the proteinGroups.txt file included in the package filepath <- system.file("extdata", "proteinGroups.txt", package = "VolcanoPlotR") # get the filename from the path filename <- basename(filepath) # get the directory name filedir <- dirname(filepath) df <- load_maxquant(file = filename, datadir = filedir) df <- process_maxquant(df, group1 = "WT", group2 = "Control") #> Using specified groups: WT versus Control # now we can generate the mean vs mean plot mean_plot_maxquant(df) # we can add a line to show the diagonal and start to label points mean_plot_maxquant(df, xy_line = TRUE, label_points = "top_20") # the parameters are the same as for the volcano plot, so we can change the colours and labels mean_plot_maxquant(df, label_points = "5_10",                    x_label = "Control (LFQ Log2)",                    y_label = "Important group (LFQ Log2)",                    vp_colours = c("0" = "#a0a000", "1" = "#808080",                                   "2" = "#606060", "3" = "#00dd80",                                   "4" = "#606060", "5" = "#800080"))

title: Outputs from VolcanoPlotR
text: can generated using pca_plot_maxquant() function. PCA plot default shows comparison runs (experimental repeats), rather proteins.  Using by_protein parameter, PCA can generated proteins instead experimental repeats. case, points coloured significance based p-value fold change thresholds. settings plot can adjusted similar ways volcano plot mean vs mean plot.
code: # pca of the experimental repeats pca_plot_maxquant(df) # pca of the proteins, coloured by significance pca_plot_maxquant(df, by_protein = TRUE)

title: Load MaxQuant output file — load_maxquant
text: Load proteinGroups.txt file MaxQuant perform cleaning steps prepare data analysis. Proteins identified site, reverse hits, potential contaminants removed, missing gene protein names filled based "Fasta.headers" column. Note: case multiple identified proteins, first one list kept.

title: Load MaxQuant output file — load_maxquant
code: load_maxquant(   file = "proteinGroups.txt",   datadir = "Data",   filepath = NULL,   clean = TRUE )

title: Load MaxQuant output file — load_maxquant
text: file character string specifying name MaxQuant output file load (default "proteinGroups.txt"). datadir character string specifying directory MaxQuant output file located (default "Data"). filepath character string specifying full path MaxQuant file. provided, override file datadir parameters. clean boolean indicating whether perform cleaning steps data (default TRUE). TRUE, rows "+" ".identified..site", "Reverse", "Potential.contaminant" columns removed, missing gene protein names filled based "Fasta.headers" column.

what: Mean Plot of Maxquant Data — mean_plot_maxquant
title: Mean Plot of Maxquant Data — mean_plot_maxquant
text: function generates plot mean value protein Group 1 (y) vs Group 2 (x) processed set MaxQuant data. data frame one passed volcano_plot_maxquant() .e. contain following columns: Protein.IDs, Gene.names, Protein.names, meas.ratio, p.value, neg.log10.p.value. function add colour code column data frame based p-value fold change thresholds, use ggplot2 create volcano plot.

title: Mean Plot of Maxquant Data — mean_plot_maxquant
code: mean_plot_maxquant(   df_subset = NULL,   meas = "LFQ.intensity",   threshold_p = 0.05,   threshold_fc = 1,   xy_line = FALSE,   vp_colours = NULL,   groups = NULL,   x_label = NULL,   y_label = NULL,   fsize = 8,   label_points = "none",   point_args = list(size = 1, shape = 16, alpha = 0.5),   label_args = list(size = 1.5, max.overlaps = 25, segment.alpha = 0.5, segment.size =     0.2, colour = "black") )

title: Mean Plot of Maxquant Data — mean_plot_maxquant
text: df_subset data frame containing subset MaxQuant data used generate plot meas string indicating measurement type (e.g. "LFQ.intensity", "Intensity", etc.) threshold_p numeric indicating p-value threshold threshold_fc numeric indicating fold change threshold (log2 space, .e. 1 2-fold change, 2 4-fold change, etc.) xy_line boolean indicating whether add line indicate x = y vp_colours named vector colours volcano plot. names vector integers 0 5, correspond different combinations p-value fold change thresholds. NULL, default colours used. groups vector two strings containing names experimental (used x-axis label). purpose allow user substitute better label used MaxQuant data. NULL values data deduced used. x_label string specifying label x-axis. NULL, default label generated based group names. y_label string specifying label y-axis. NULL, default label generated. fsize numeric indicating font size use plot (default 8) label_points string indicating points label plot. Options "none" (default) labels, "" (attempt label points), character vector selected proteins label (e.g. c("P12345", "Q67890", "IPO5") can Gene.names, Protein.names, Protein.ID mix values used label), code auto-labelling (e.g. "3_all" "5_10" label significantly de-enriched points (colour code 3) top 10 significantly enriched proteins (colour code 5), code "top_20" label top 20 proteins manhattan distance regardless colour code). point_args list arguments passed geom_point() points volcano plot (default list(size = 1, shape = 16, alpha = 0.5)). user specifies additional arguments changes default argument, defaults merged user-specified entries. label_args list arguments passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel() labels volcano plot (default list(size = 1.5, max.overlaps = 25, segment.alpha = 0.5, segment.size = 0.2, colour = "black")). user specifies additional arguments changes default argument, defaults merged user-specified entries.

text: ggplot object containing volcano plot

what: PCA Plot of MaxQuant Sample Columns — pca_plot_maxquant
title: PCA Plot of MaxQuant Sample Columns — pca_plot_maxquant
text: function performs principal component analysis measurement columns present processed MaxQuant subset plots PC1 PC2, sample column shown point. input data frame processed subset passed volcano_plot_maxquant().

title: PCA Plot of MaxQuant Sample Columns — pca_plot_maxquant
code: pca_plot_maxquant(   df_subset = NULL,   meas = "LFQ.intensity",   groups = NULL,   by_protein = FALSE,   threshold_p = 0.05,   threshold_fc = 1,   vp_colours = NULL,   x_label = NULL,   y_label = NULL,   xy_line = TRUE,   fsize = 8,   label_points = NULL,   point_args = list(size = 3, alpha = 0.8),   label_args = list(size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf, segment.alpha = 0.5, segment.size =     0.2, colour = "black") )

title: PCA Plot of MaxQuant Sample Columns — pca_plot_maxquant
text: df_subset data frame containing subset MaxQuant data used generate plot meas string indicating measurement type (e.g. "LFQ.intensity", "Intensity", etc.) groups optional character vector used relabel experimental groups legend. NULL, group names deduced measurement column names. by_protein boolean indicating whether perform PCA protein rows (default FALSE). TRUE, PCA performed protein rows rather sample columns. threshold_p numeric indicating p-value threshold. Used by_protein TRUE colour points PCA plot based significance. threshold_fc numeric indicating fold change threshold (log2 space, .e. 1 2-fold change, 2 4-fold change, etc.). Used by_protein TRUE colour points PCA plot based significance. vp_colours named vector colours PCA plot. x_label string specifying label x-axis. NULL, default label showing variance explained PC1 generated. y_label string specifying label y-axis. NULL, default label showing variance explained PC2 generated. xy_line boolean indicating whether add line indicate x = y = 0 fsize numeric indicating font size use plot (default 8) label_points boolean indicating whether label sample points (default NULL triggers setting FALSE by_protein TRUE, TRUE by_protein FALSE). TRUE, sample points labelled sample names. FALSE, labels added. point_args list arguments passed geom_point() points PCA plot (default list(size = 3, alpha = 0.8)). user specifies additional arguments changes default argument, defaults merged user-specified entries. label_args list arguments passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel() labels PCA plot (default list(size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf, segment.alpha = 0.5, segment.size = 0.2, colour = "black")). user specifies additional arguments changes default argument, defaults merged user-specified entries.

title: PCA Plot of MaxQuant Sample Columns — pca_plot_maxquant
text: ggplot object containing PCA plot

what: Process MaxQuant data for volcano plot — process_maxquant
title: Process MaxQuant data for volcano plot — process_maxquant
text: function takes data frame containing MaxQuant output processes prepare volcano plot. identifies experimental groups, allows user select groups compare, imputes missing values, calculates log2 fold changes p-values protein, returns processed data frame ready plotting. uses equivalent processing Perseus, including imputation missing values Gaussian distribution specified downshift width parameters. Note, group1 compared group2, log2 fold change calculated group1 - group2, meaning positive value indicates enrichment group1 negative value indicates enrichment group2.

title: Process MaxQuant data for volcano plot — process_maxquant
code: process_maxquant(   df = NULL,   group1 = NULL,   group2 = NULL,   meas = "LFQ.intensity",   baseval = 0,   width = 0.3,   downshift = 1.8,   seed = 123,   var.equal = TRUE,   paired = FALSE )

title: Process MaxQuant data for volcano plot — process_maxquant
text: df data frame containing MaxQuant output, typically loaded using load_maxquant(). group1 character string specifying name first experimental group compare (e.g., "Treatment"). NULL, user prompted select groups found data frame. group2 character string specifying name second experimental group compare (e.g., "Control"). NULL, user prompted select groups found data frame. meas character string specifying prefix measurement columns use comparison (default "LFQ.intensity"). baseval numeric value specifying base value impute missing values (default 0). typically 0 MaxQuant LFQ intensity data, may different types data. width numeric value specifying width parameter imputation missing values Gaussian distribution (default 0.3). typically 0.3 MaxQuant LFQ intensity data, may different types data. downshift numeric value specifying downshift parameter imputation missing values Gaussian distribution (default 1.8). typically 1.8 MaxQuant LFQ intensity data, may different types data. seed numeric value specifying random seed use imputation missing values Gaussian distribution (default 123). Setting seed ensures imputation reproducible. var.equal boolean indicating whether assume equal variances t-test (default TRUE). typically TRUE MaxQuant LFQ intensity data, may different types data. paired boolean indicating whether perform paired t-test (default FALSE ). Note, paired assumes columns group1 group2 order correspond . check made match columns name.

text: data frame containing processed MaxQuant data suitable volcano plot visualization, including columns log2 fold change p-values.

title: Process multiple MaxQuant datasets for volcano plot — process_multiple_maxquant
text: data list data frame containing MaxQuant outputs, typically loaded using load_multiple_maxquant(). group1 character string specifying name first experimental group compare (e.g., "Treatment"). NULL, user prompted select groups found data frame. group2 character string specifying name second experimental group compare (e.g., "Control"). NULL, user prompted select groups found data frame. meas character string specifying prefix measurement columns use comparison (default "LFQ.intensity"). baseval numeric value specifying base value impute missing values (default 0). typically 0 MaxQuant LFQ intensity data, may different types data. width numeric value specifying width parameter imputation missing values Gaussian distribution (default 0.3). typically 0.3 MaxQuant LFQ intensity data, may different types data. downshift numeric value specifying downshift parameter imputation missing values Gaussian distribution (default 1.8). typically 1.8 MaxQuant LFQ intensity data, may different types data. seed numeric value specifying random seed use imputation missing values Gaussian distribution (default 123). Setting seed ensures imputation reproducible. var.equal boolean indicating whether assume equal variances t-test (default TRUE). typically TRUE MaxQuant LFQ intensity data, may different types data. paired boolean indicating whether perform paired t-test (default FALSE ). Note, paired assumes columns group1 group2 order correspond . check made match columns name. ratio boolean indicating whether enrichment group 1 vs group 2 calculated protein run averaged (default FALSE). FALSE, mean measure group 1 protein compared mean measure group 2. ratio method useful files processed different batches means groups comparable. ratio method robust batch effects, requires number replicates group 1 group 2 .

text: function generates volcano plot subset MaxQuant data. data frame contain following columns: Protein.IDs, Gene.names, Protein.names, meas.ratio, p.value, neg.log10.p.value. function add colour code column data frame based p-value fold change thresholds, use ggplot2 create volcano plot.

title: Volcano Plot of Maxquant Data — volcano_plot_maxquant
code: volcano_plot_maxquant(   df_subset = NULL,   meas = "LFQ.intensity",   threshold_p = 0.05,   threshold_fc = 1,   p_line = TRUE,   zero_line = TRUE,   x_line = FALSE,   vp_colours = NULL,   groups = NULL,   x_label = NULL,   y_label = NULL,   fsize = 8,   text_output = FALSE,   text_output_dir = "Output/Data",   label_points = "none",   point_args = list(size = 1, shape = 16, alpha = 0.5),   label_args = list(size = 1.5, max.overlaps = 25, segment.alpha = 0.5, segment.size =     0.2, colour = "black") )

title: Volcano Plot of Maxquant Data — volcano_plot_maxquant
text: df_subset data frame containing subset MaxQuant data used generate plot meas string indicating measurement type (e.g. "LFQ.intensity", "Intensity", etc.) threshold_p numeric indicating p-value threshold threshold_fc numeric indicating fold change threshold (log2 space, .e. 1 2-fold change, 2 4-fold change, etc.) p_line boolean indicating whether add horizontal line p-value threshold zero_line boolean indicating whether add vertical line x=0 x_line boolean indicating whether add vertical lines fold change thresholds vp_colours named vector colours volcano plot. names vector integers 0 5, correspond different combinations p-value fold change thresholds. NULL, default colours used. groups vector two strings containing names experimental (used x-axis label). purpose allow user substitute better label used MaxQuant data. NULL values data deduced used. x_label string specifying label x-axis. NULL, default label generated based group names. y_label string specifying label y-axis. NULL, default label generated. fsize numeric indicating font size use plot (default 8) text_output boolean indicating whether save ranked protein list text file (default FALSE). text_output_dir character string specifying directory optional text file saved(default "Output/Data/"). label_points string indicating points label plot. Options "none" (default) labels, "" (attempt label points), character vector selected proteins label (e.g. c("P12345", "Q67890", "IPO5") can Gene.names, Protein.names, Protein.ID mix values used label), code auto-labelling (e.g. "3_all" "5_10" label significantly de-enriched points (colour code 3) top 10 significantly enriched proteins (colour code 5), code "top_20" label top 20 proteins manhattan distance regardless colour code). point_args list arguments passed geom_point() points volcano plot (default list(size = 1, shape = 16, alpha = 0.5)). user specifies additional arguments changes default argument, defaults merged user-specified entries. label_args list arguments passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel() labels volcano plot (default list(size = 1.5, max.overlaps = 25, segment.alpha = 0.5, segment.size = 0.2, colour = "black")). user specifies additional arguments changes default argument, defaults merged user-specified entries.

title: Volcano Plot of Maxquant Data — volcano_plot_maxquant
text: ggplot object containing volcano plot

title: Volcano Plot of Maxquant Data — volcano_plot_maxquant
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # load the MaxQuant data df <- load_maxquant() # process the MaxQuant data to get the subset for the volcano plot df_subset <- process_maxquant(df, group1 = "Treatment", group2  = "Control") # generate the volcano plot volcano_plot_maxquant(df_subset) } # }

what: Workflow for MaxQuant data analysis — workflow_maxquant
title: Workflow for MaxQuant data analysis — workflow_maxquant
text: purpose function provide single function take proteinGroups.txt produced MaxQuant, perform necessary data processing, generate volcano plot. function call load_maxquant(), process_maxquant(), volcano_plot_maxquant() functions turn. user asked specify groups group 1 group 2.

title: Workflow for MaxQuant data analysis — workflow_maxquant
code: workflow_maxquant(...)

title: Workflow for MaxQuant data analysis — workflow_maxquant
text: ... ellipsis allow user specify arguments function workflow. accepted arguments function follows: load_maxquant(): file, datadir, clean process_maxquant(): group1, group2, meas, baseval, width, downshift, seed, var.equal, paired volcano_plot_maxquant(): meas, threshold_p, threshold_fc, p_line, zero_line, x_line, vp_colours, groups, x_label, y_label, fsize

title: Workflow for MaxQuant data analysis — workflow_maxquant
